page: Amazon Web Services m7gd.16xlarge is a general-purpose server powered by custom AWS Graviton processors built on the 64-bit ARM64 architecture. It features 64 dedicated physical cores running at 2.6 GHz with no hyperthreading. The memory configuration consists of 256 GB of RAM, yielding a balanced 4 GB per core ratio. Local storage is bundled, providing 3800 GB of high-speed NVMe SSD instance storage, and the instance operates on the AWS Nitro hypervisor with a baseline network bandwidth of 30 Gbps.

In benchmark testing, the m7gd.16xlarge demonstrates strong multi-core performance, achieving a top-tier Geekbench multi-core score of 20645.0 and a strong stress-ng multi-core CPU score of 97895.22. Memory subsystem performance is highly competitive, with a top-tier Passmark memory score and a read-only small block cached memory bandwidth of 6195.29 GB/sec. Software compilation using Clang also registers in the top tier. For database and key-value operations, the server shows strong capabilities, executing 102.0 million Redis SET operations per second and scoring highly in Passmark database operations.

The qualitative cost efficiency of the m7gd.16xlarge is driven by its ARM-based architecture and high resource density, offering a favorable performance-per-resource profile compared to traditional x86 architectures. However, a notable tradeoff is its single-core performance and certain specialized tasks; for instance, LLM text generation with small models is relatively weak at 61.82 tokens per second, though medium model text generation remains strong. Given its balanced compute-to-memory ratio, high-speed local NVMe storage, and strong multi-core throughput, this server is suitable for database hosting, static web serving, software compilation pipelines, and general-purpose containerized workloads.
description: The Amazon Web Services m7gd.16xlarge is an ARM64-based general-purpose server powered by a 64-core AWS Graviton processor running at 2.6 GHz. It features 256 GB of RAM, 3800 GB of local NVMe SSD instance storage, and a baseline network bandwidth of 30 Gbps. Operating on the Nitro hypervisor, the instance delivers strong multi-core performance, highlighted by top-tier Geekbench multi-core and software compilation scores. Its memory subsystem is highly efficient, achieving top-tier Passmark memory ratings and strong read/write bandwidth. While single-core and small-model LLM text generation benchmarks are moderate to weak, the server excels in multi-threaded tasks, database operations, and high-throughput web serving. The m7gd.16xlarge offers a qualitatively cost-effective profile for resource-dense workloads, making it appropriate for database hosting, CI/CD compilation pipelines, and general-purpose enterprise applications requiring fast local storage.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m7gd.16xlarge is an ARM64 general-purpose server featuring 64 Graviton physical cores, 256 GB of RAM, 3800 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, and 30 Gbps baseline network bandwidth.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services m7gd.16xlarge is an ARM64 general-purpose server featuring 64 Graviton cores, 256 GB of RAM, and 3800 GB of local NVMe SSD storage.
tagline: A balanced ARM64 server featuring dedicated physical cores, high-speed local NVMe storage, and strong multi-core memory bandwidth.
bullet_points: 64 dedicated ARM64 Graviton physical cores running at 2.6 GHz
256 GB RAM paired with 3800 GB of local NVMe SSD storage
Top-tier multi-core CPU and software compilation benchmark performance
Strong database execution capabilities, including 102.0 million Redis SET operations per second
30 Gbps baseline network bandwidth powered by the Nitro hypervisor
Optimized for database hosting, CI/CD pipelines, and static web serving
